TEAMWORK: Communication (call for ball, signal plays), Trust (rely on teammates), Roles (everyone has a position/function), Shared goals (team wins together, loses together). Sport builds resilience — how you respond to losing matters more than the loss itself.
key_facts: Team sports teach communication and trust
Every player has a specific role

subjects.name: Social Studies
subjects.desc: Understand society, culture, and the world we share

topics.title: Our Community
topics.intro: A community is a group of people who live and work together in the same place and care for each other!
topics.content: Every community has people with different jobs who help each other. Doctors keep us healthy. Teachers help us learn. Police officers keep us safe. Firefighters respond to emergencies. Supermarket workers make sure we have food. Bus drivers take us places. Everyone in a community depends on everyone else! Good citizens are responsible: follow rules, help neighbors, keep public spaces clean, vote when old enough, pay taxes, respect others. A strong community is one where people look out for each other.
key_facts: Good citizens follow rules and help others
Communities can be local, national, or global

topics.title: How Economies Work
topics.intro: An economy is the system by which goods and services are produced, distributed, and consumed. It affects everyone!
topics.content: KEY CONCEPTS: Supply (how much of something is available), Demand (how much people want it). Price is determined by supply and demand balance. GDP (Gross Domestic Product) measures a country's total economic output. Inflation is the general rise in prices over time. Unemployment rate: % of workforce without jobs. ECONOMIC SYSTEMS: Free market/Capitalism (private ownership, profit motive), Socialism (government controls key industries), Mixed economy (most real countries — elements of both). Trade: countries specialize in what they do best and trade the rest. Financial crises show how interconnected economies are.

subjects.name: Hindi
subjects.desc: Learn Hindi — India's most widely spoken language

topics.title: Devanagari Script — Vowels
topics.intro: Hindi is written in Devanagari script. It has 11 vowels and 33 consonants — very logical and phonetic!
topics.content: Devanagari script is phonetic — it's written as it sounds! VOWELS (स्वर): अ (a), आ (aa), इ (i), ई (ii), उ (u), ऊ (uu), ए (e), ऐ (ai), ओ (o), औ (au), अं (an). Each vowel has a full form and a vowel marker (matra) used when attached to consonants. Hindi is written left to right, with words connected by a horizontal line on top (शिरोरेखा). Hindi is spoken by ~600 million people as a first or second language. It's the official language of India along with English.

topics.title: Hindi — Greetings and Phrases
topics.intro: नमस्ते (Namaste)! Let's learn essential Hindi greetings and phrases!
topics.content: GREETINGS: नमस्ते Namaste (Hello/respectful greeting with hands pressed together), सुप्रभात Suprabhat (Good morning), शुभ रात्रि Shubh Ratri (Good night), अलविदा Alvida (Goodbye). USEFUL PHRASES: आपका नाम क्या है? Aapka naam kya hai? (What is your name?), मेरा नाम... है Mera naam... hai (My name is...), धन्यवाद Dhanyavaad (Thank you), कृपया Kripaya (Please), क्षमा करें Kshama karein (Sorry/Excuse me), हाँ Haan (Yes), नहीं Nahin (No). NUMBERS 1-5: एक ek (1), दो do (2), तीन teen (3), चार chaar (4), पाँच paanch (5).
key_facts: Numbers: ek, do, teen, chaar, paanch

subjects.name: Indian Studies
subjects.desc: Discover India's rich history, culture, and achievements

topics.title: India's Ancient History
topics.intro: India is home to one of the world's oldest civilizations — the Indus Valley Civilization, over 4,000 years old!
topics.content: INDUS VALLEY CIVILIZATION (3300-1300 BCE): One of the world's earliest urban civilizations in modern Pakistan/NW India. Cities like Mohenjo-daro and Harappa had advanced drainage systems, planned streets, and writing. VEDIC PERIOD: Aryans brought Sanskrit language and the Vedas (ancient scriptures). MAURYA EMPIRE (322-185 BCE): Chandragupta Maurya unified India; Ashoka the Great spread Buddhism and promoted peace. GUPTA EMPIRE (320-550 CE): 'Golden Age' — advances in mathematics (concept of zero!), astronomy, literature, and art. India's civilization continuously shaped Asia and the world.
key_facts: Indus Valley: one of the world's oldest civilizations
Concept of zero invented in India

topics.title: Indian Independence Movement
topics.intro: India's freedom struggle against British rule was led by Mahatma Gandhi through non-violence — and changed the world!
topics.content: Britain controlled India from 1858 (after the 1857 uprising). The Indian National Congress was founded in 1885. Key figures: Mahatma Gandhi (satyagraha — non-violent resistance, salt march 1930, Quit India 1942), Jawaharlal Nehru (first PM), Subhas Chandra Bose (advocated armed resistance), Bhagat Singh, Bal Gangadhar Tilak, Dr B.R. Ambedkar (drafted Indian constitution, fought for Dalit rights). India gained independence on August 15, 1947, but was partitioned into India and Pakistan — causing massive displacement and violence. Gandhi was assassinated on January 30, 1948.
key_facts: Partition of India and Pakistan caused violence

subjects.name: Tamil
subjects.desc: Learn Tamil — one of the world's oldest classical languages

topics.title: Tamil Script and Basics
topics.intro: Tamil is over 2,000 years old — one of the world's oldest living languages! It has a beautiful script and rich literature.
topics.content: Tamil is a classical language with over 2,000 years of literature. It's spoken by ~80 million people in Tamil Nadu (India), Sri Lanka, Singapore, and Malaysia. THE SCRIPT: Tamil has 12 vowels, 18 consonants, and 1 special character — combinations give 247 characters. BASICS: வணக்கம் Vanakkam (Hello/Greetings), நன்றி Nandri (Thank you), ஆமாம் Aamam (Yes), இல்லை Illai (No). NUMBERS 1-5: ஒன்று ondru (1), இரண்டு irandu (2), மூன்று moondru (3), நான்கு naangu (4), ஐந்து aindhu (5).
